title: scanf() and fscanf() format strings should specify a field width for the %s string placeholder
description: By default, there is no limit on the length of the string being read. The scanf family of functions will continue to read characters into the buffer until they encounter a whitespace character. If the input contains a string that is long enough and lacks whitespace characters, it can result in memory beyond the end of the buffer being overwritten.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: scanf family functions with %s format specifier without field width detected. Specify field width to prevent buffer overflow.
Simple %s format without field width in scanf family functions. Use %[number]s to limit input length.
Format string contains %s with whitespace. Specify field width like %19s to prevent buffer overflow.
Variadic scanf functions with unbounded %s format specifier detected. Specify field width for safety.
Format string uses %*s (assignment suppression) without field width. Consider %*19s to limit input.
Multiple format specifiers with unbounded %s detected. All %s should have field width specified.
Character class %[...]s format without field width detected. Use %[number][...]s for bounded input.
Multiple %s format specifiers in single format string without field widths. Each %s needs field width.
Wide string format %ls without field width detected. Use %[number]ls to limit wide string input.
